name: Region Interconnection Graph
description: Global map of proximity, trade corridors, alliance overlaps, and dependency chains across all tracked regions

europe.notes: EU is the regulatory and trade hub; eurozone subset shares monetary policy; Nordic Council and Visegrad are sub-regional blocs; Ruhr Area is the industrial core; Baltic Sea is a shared maritime zone with NATO significance

asia_pacific.notes: ASEAN is the diplomatic hub; RCEP is the trade framework; Quad and AUKUS are security arrangements; Greater Bay Area, Pearl River Delta, and Yangtze River Delta are manufacturing clusters; South China Sea and Taiwan Strait are contested maritime zones

middle_east.notes: GCC anchors the Gulf economies; OPEC/OPEC+ set global oil output; Strait of Hormuz is the critical oil chokepoint; Levant is a conflict-prone zone; Bosphorus connects Black Sea trade to Mediterranean; Red Sea links Suez to Indian Ocean

africa.notes: African Union is the continental body; ECOWAS covers West Africa trade; EAC covers East Africa; SADC covers Southern Africa; Sahel and Horn of Africa are instability zones; Cape of Good Hope is the alternative route when Suez is disrupted

americas.notes: NAFTA/USMCA is the dominant trade framework; Mercosur covers South American trade; Corn Belt and Rust Belt are key US production zones; Silicon Valley is the global tech hub; Panama Canal connects Atlantic and Pacific shipping

maritime_corridors.notes: These chokepoints and sea lanes carry the bulk of global trade; Strait of Malacca alone handles ~25% of global shipping; disruption at any point forces costly rerouting and supply chain delays

global_blocs.notes: G20 is the primary forum for global economic coordination; G7 represents advanced economies; BRICS is the emerging-market counterweight; Five Eyes is the core Anglophone intelligence alliance; SCO is the China-Russia led security bloc; CPTPP and RCEP are competing Asia-Pacific trade frameworks; Belt and Road is China's infrastructure investment network

critical_corridors.cargo: containers, electronics, manufactured goods, auto parts
critical_corridors.notes: Primary artery for China-EU trade; ~12,000 nm; 30-35 day transit; Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping in 2024 forced diversions via Cape of Good Hope
Carries ~17 million barrels/day of crude; critical energy lifeline for China, Japan, South Korea, India
Key European energy supply route; alternative pipeline routes via Turkey reduce but do not eliminate dependence
Transpacific route; Taiwan Strait tension directly threatens semiconductor supply; ~14-day transit to US West Coast ports

critical_corridors.cargo: LNG, crude oil, agricultural products, chemicals
critical_corridors.notes: Transatlantic trade route; US became net energy exporter; key for European energy diversification from Russia
Brazil-China commodity corridor; also routes via Panama Canal for lighter cargo; critical for Chinese food and industrial input security
Panama Canal handles ~5% of global seaborne trade; drought restrictions in 2023-24 reduced daily transits from ~38 to ~24
Ukraine/Russia grain exports transit Black Sea; Bosphorus is governed by 1936 Montreux Convention; Turkey controls access
Cuts East Asia-Europe transit by ~10 days vs Suez route; navigable July-November; Russia controls most of the route; ice retreat expanding seasonal window
Gulf of Guinea is a major oil production zone; piracy risk; ECOWAS instability affects supply reliability
Australia is China's largest iron ore supplier; trade tensions led to coal diversion to India and other markets

rivalry_axes.notes: Contest over Indo-Pacific primacy, technology supremacy (semiconductors, AI), trade rules, and alliance networks; Taiwan is the most acute flashpoint
Ukraine war is the active conflict; NATO expansion to Finland/Sweden reshaped Baltic security; energy decoupling from Russia redirected global LNG flows; Arctic is emerging theater

rivalry_axes.nature: regional power struggle and proxy conflicts
rivalry_axes.notes: Iran's proxy network (Hezbollah, Houthis, militias in Iraq) threatens Gulf shipping and Israeli security; Houthi Red Sea attacks demonstrated cascading global trade impact

rivalry_axes.nature: border disputes and competing influence in South/Southeast Asia
rivalry_axes.notes: Himalayan border standoff since 2020; competing infrastructure investments in Indian Ocean region; India excluded from RCEP partly due to China concerns

rivalry_axes.nature: territorial disputes over islands, reefs, and exclusive economic zones
rivalry_axes.notes: Nine-dash line claims overlap with Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, Brunei EEZs; militarized artificial islands; risk of disruption to $3.4 trillion annual shipping through South China Sea

rivalry_axes.nature: governance collapse, coups, jihadist insurgency, great power competition
rivalry_axes.notes: Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger military coups; Russian Wagner/Africa Corps replacing French influence; migration pressure on Europe; disruption to gold and uranium mining

rivalry_axes.nature: competing visions for global economic governance
rivalry_axes.notes: BRICS expansion (2024: Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, UAE, Saudi Arabia joined) challenges dollar-centric system; push for alternative payment systems and development banks

dependency_chains.notes: Asia imports ~75% of Persian Gulf crude; Hormuz closure would remove ~20% of global oil supply; strategic petroleum reserves provide ~90 days buffer
TSMC produces ~90% of sub-7nm chips; disruption would halt smartphone, automotive, AI, and defense production globally; US CHIPS Act and EU Chips Act aim to reduce dependency

dependency_chains.chain: Black Sea grain → MENA / Africa food security
dependency_chains.notes: Ukraine and Russia supply ~30% of global wheat exports; disruption causes acute food insecurity in import-dependent MENA and African nations
Australia supplies ~60% of China's iron ore; China produces ~55% of global steel; disruption cascades to global construction, manufacturing, and infrastructure

dependency_chains.chain: Corn Belt / Mercosur soybeans → China feed
dependency_chains.notes: China imports ~60% of globally traded soybeans; US-China trade war shifted sourcing to Brazil; critical for Chinese livestock feed and food security
Post-Russia pivot: Europe increased LNG imports from Qatar and US; Qatar's North Field expansion targets European market; spot LNG prices highly sensitive to shipping disruptions
DRC produces ~70% of global cobalt; critical for EV batteries and electronics; artisanal mining and conflict complicate supply; China controls ~60% of refining

Nord Stream pipelines sabotaged 2022; pipeline flows reduced ~80% from pre-war levels; remaining flows via Ukraine transit (contract expired 2024) and TurkStream; structural European energy cost increase
OPEC+ controls ~40% of global oil production; output cuts/increases directly affect fuel costs, transportation, fertilizer prices, and headline inflation globally

dependency_chains.chain: Belt and Road infrastructure → emerging market debt
dependency_chains.notes: China's BRI has deployed ~$1 trillion in loans and investments across 150+ countries; creates trade corridors but also debt dependency; port and rail projects reshape trade flows

eu_eurozone.notes: Eurozone is a subset of EU (20 of 27 members share the euro); EU non-eurozone members retain own currencies

opec_gcc.notes: Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait are in all three; OPEC+ includes Russia and other non-OPEC producers; GCC includes non-OPEC Bahrain and Oman

quad_aukus_five_eyes.notes: Australia is in all three; US is in Quad and Five Eyes; UK is in AUKUS and Five Eyes; overlapping Indo-Pacific security architecture

rcep_asean_cptpp.notes: All 10 ASEAN members are in RCEP; 7 ASEAN members are in CPTPP; RCEP includes China (CPTPP does not yet); CPTPP includes Western Hemisphere members

brics_sco.notes: China, Russia, India are in both; SCO is security-focused, BRICS is economic; both serve as counterweight forums to Western institutions

g7_g20.notes: All G7 members (US, UK, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, Canada + EU) are in G20; G20 adds China, India, Brazil, Russia, Australia, South Korea, Indonesia, Mexico, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Argentina, South Africa

eu_nordic_visegrad.notes: Nordic Council includes EU members (Sweden, Denmark, Finland) and non-EU (Norway, Iceland); all four Visegrad members (Poland, Czech Republic, Slovakia, Hungary) are EU members

persian_gulf_hormuz_gcc.notes: Geographically overlapping; all GCC states border the Persian Gulf; Strait of Hormuz is the exit point; Iran borders all three but is not in GCC

african_union_sub_blocs.notes: ECOWAS (West Africa), EAC (East Africa), SADC (Southern Africa) are all sub-blocs under the African Union umbrella; AfCFTA aims to unify them into a continental free trade area

nafta_usmca_cptpp.notes: Canada and Mexico are in both; US is in USMCA but withdrew from TPP/CPTPP; creates overlapping and sometimes conflicting trade rules

cis_sco_opec_plus.notes: Russia is in all three; Kazakhstan is in CIS and SCO; Central Asian energy producers bridge these blocs

red_sea_suez_mediterranean.notes: Sequential maritime zones forming the Europe-Asia sea corridor; disruption in any one forces rerouting via Cape of Good Hope adding 10-14 days transit

greater_bay_pearl_river_yangtze.notes: Greater Bay Area encompasses Pearl River Delta plus Hong Kong and Macau; Yangtze River Delta (Shanghai-centered) is the other major Chinese economic cluster; together they produce ~35% of China's GDP

belt_and_road_cis_sco.notes: Central Asian BRI corridors run through CIS and SCO member states; China-Europe rail freight transits Kazakhstan and Russia

corn_belt_rust_belt.notes: Adjacent US regions; Corn Belt (Iowa, Illinois, Indiana) is agricultural; Rust Belt (Michigan, Ohio, Pennsylvania) is industrial; both are politically pivotal swing regions
